profiles.name: Takashi Amemiya
profiles.title: Senior Vice President, Mizuho Financial Group
profiles.bio: Takashi is a senior vice president of Mizuho Financial Group, a Japanese banking group, with an 18-year career. He has in-depth knowledge and extensive experience spanning general management, strategy, marketing and sales in retail banking and asset management business. Takashi managed a project team consisting of 30 internal and external experts from October 2013 to March 2016 and built the foundation of Mizuho's asset management business strategy. His empathetic, collaborative leadership style and conceptual thinking were further refined in the process of leading the diverse members of the project team to design a long-term sustainable business strategy. In the Asset Management Division, as team leader, Takashi was instrumental in a) coordinating collaboration among Mizuho's banking, trust, securities and asset management functions, b) developing the annual and mid-term business strategy, c) allocating and controlling the annual budget and human resources, d) enhancing relationships with institutional investors and financial authorities, and e) managing the progress of the banking account system renewal project. Additionally, owing to his desire for better working conditions and healthier and more productive lives, he was elected as a Mizuho Financial Group Trade Union representative from 26,000 members from 2011 to 2013. As a union manager, he was responsible for many Mizuho Bank and Trust Bank workplaces, working on the development of a friendly working environment for the members. He also planned and operated events for enriching leisure time, promoting interaction between the members and self-development.
career_highlights: Led the project team and proposed to executives necessary changes for the future growth on a) a historical view of asset management business in Japan, b) expansion of long-term diversified investment for individuals, c) the use of digital technology, and d) the ideal asset management company.
Made a significant contribution to the long-term stability of clients' pension funds by developing an optimal pension sales structure and improving service delivery.

Managed the management and post-merger integration of Asset Management One, which was created by the merger of three companies in 2016 and had ¥57 trillion AUM as of March 2021. Takashi Amemiya Senior Vice President, Mizuho Financial Group

She collaborated with the senior leaders globally to develop solutions and new strategies to improve efficiencies, control costs and drive customer acquisition and retention. Prior Marriott, she was responsible for the marketing strategy of the international division of STR, the global leader in hotel benchmarking. She successfully developed the international brand presence of the newly formed company and commented frequently on hotel performance trends at industry conferences and in the media. The past roles enabled her to build upon her curiosity, creativity and ability to bring together different perspectives to get results. She is a thought-leader moving easily from the big picture to the detail and takes initiative to enhance business performance. A collaborator who together with senior stakeholders from various functions seeks solutions for business-critical challenges, gains alignment and drives change. Konstanze enjoys working in a fast-paced environment, working with cross-cultural teams to achieve excellent results for her clients. Konstanze supports her family's art gallery and holiday apartment business and engages in her local community to enhance the wellbeing within it. She speaks English, German, and conversational French; and has lived in UK, Germany, and USA.

Prepared analysis, insights and ideas to formulate key strategic responses to business challenges; developed and aligned on the three year strategy for Sales & Marketing disciplines in Europe for Marriott International.

Built up brand and analytical capability in newly-formed company to gain global leadership. Respected and trusted commentator on performance trends to financiers, owners, operators and consultants in the hospitality industry. Konstanze Auernheimer Planning, Strategy & Transformation leader

She is a curious, growth-minded, and knowledge-seeking individual. Her biggest strengths are relationship management & leading multi-cultural teams. She is extremely passionate about giving back to the less privileged. She has been working with blind women's non-profit of 3000+ blind women with household incomes of ~$50 per month since she was 18. In addition, she is a globetrotter & passionate traveller, trained folk dancer, and amateur wine connoisseur.

profiles.name: Daniel Carvalho

He has a track record of structuring new operations, managing crises, operations turnaround, and defining new standards and process strategies in a dynamic and uncertain environment. Moreover, he fully understands how diversity plays a capital role while designing new teams, making inclusion and diversity a core pillar in the work environment to promote agile and sustainable changes. Daniel likes people and benefits from his natural curiosity and interpersonal skills to establish strong internal and external networks with associations, other market players, and worldwide top knowledge hubs to bring together individual ambitions and the corporations strategic goals. As a PE/VC investor, she managed a portfolio of high potential businesses and a family of investment funds that channeled smart capital into key growth sectors. In her combined role as Partner and Investment Principal, she made investments in companies that earned a commercial return but also provided positive social/development impact. Post investment, via board level representation and non- financial support, she worked to create value by playing an active role in the companies' development and the evolution of their strategies. Recently, Mobola had the privilege of co-creating a pioneering gender-focused fund to invest in businesses in West and Southern Africa that have a significant impact on the lives of women. She has developed innovative interventions in the renewable energy space, working with multinationals and international funders to catalyze investment in sustainable off-grid energy solutions. Mobola has been an active participant in the advancement of Africa's investment ecosystem. She collaborated with other stakeholders to birth Nigeria's first formal angel investing network and led the birth of a non-profit entrepreneur community to promote entrepreneurship as a catalyst for transforming lives. She has invested in building a deep network of business associates and spoken at conferences and panels about various topics concerning investing and development.

profiles.name: Sandeep Dama
profiles.title: Entrepreneur and Board Member
profiles.bio: Sandeep is a serial entrepreneur with over 14 years of experience in establishing, building and leading innovative businesses across financial services, electric mobility, real estate and manufacturing sectors. In addition, he has hands-on experience in raising funds, investing and sale of businesses successfully. He has significant skills in leadership, growth, turnaround, financial and operational skills. He is also adept at building high-performance teams and growing organisations through mentoring and support. Sandeep has founded and run businesses with P&L responsibility and focus on business growth and profitability. In addition, he has experience in setting up international supply chains and distribution networks. Sandeep began his career as an M&A banker with Rothschild in their corporate finance advisory teams in London and later in Mumbai, India. As part of these teams, he was involved in several landmark deals across various sectors, including infrastructure, real estate and media. Apart from his entrepreneurial role, Sandeep also serves on the board of a large educational institution in India, leading the startup and entrepreneurship initiatives. He also previously worked on the board of a charitable school in Bangalore, India. Sandeep has moved into a non-executive role at his organisations after joining the Sloan program.

Founded and setup electric vehicle company with manufacturing capacity of 200,000 units per year.

profiles.name: Della-Moretta
profiles.title: Managing Consultant, Gobeyond Partners
profiles.bio: Over the course of two decades, Leo has been driven to help organizations solve their most difficult problems. Leo's innate curiosity has led to him to take on diverse projects in a wide variety of arenas. The breadth of his experience includes serving as a strategic planner for the US Military, a Non-Profit Fellow working in poverty alleviation, and as a strategy and transformation consultation for leading international organizations. This unique background has made Leo an award-winning innovator with a track record of bridging design, strategy, analytics, and operations. In his six years as a consultant, Leo has been instrumental in delivering transformations for his FTSE 100 clients in the UK, USA, Mexico, Brazil, Argentina, Colombia, and Italy. Key to Leo's success is his inclusive approach to leading and facilitating multicultural and multidisciplinary teams, allowing the creation and implementation of highly effective solutions. As an engagement manager with client ownership, Leo believes it is possible to create an environment that fosters innovation and encourages creativity, while simultaneously ensuring that the client's brief is exceeded. Leo began his career as an officer in the US Army where he led organizations ranging from 45 -1500 people in the USA, Europe, and Asia. Following his military service, Leo took a gap year and working as a Kiva fellow, helping women and the LGBT community in Ecuador access micro-finance loans. Since finishing his MBA and his Parsons MSc, his career has focused on helping companies transform their customer experiences a via a design-led approach.

profiles.name: Finance Director, Operations and Transformation,
profiles.title: O2 UK Board Director, CTIL

Before joining the Sloan Programme at LBS, Laura was Finance Director for Operations and Transformation and Chair of the Digital Transformation Programme Management Board at O2 UK. Laura was also a Board Director of CTIL, O2's 50:50 network sharing joint venture with Vodafone since 2017. Prior to this, she was Head of Strategic Planning at O2 UK. In 2013, she was appointed Group Financial Controller for Telefonica Europe and served as Head of Investment for Telefonica Europe. Laura held different positions in the Finance, Strategy and Engineering areas of Telefonica, both at Telefonica Latin America in Spain and Telefonica de Argentina from 2001 to 2011. Her career started in the Business Division of Alcatel in 2000. An Electronic Engineer by training, she graduated from the Buenos Aires Institute of Technology (Argentina) and holds an International MBA from IE Business School (Spain). In addition, she has attended Executive Development programmes at Stanford GSB (US) and LBS (UK). Laura has consistently been part of the talent pool of Telefonica and was awarded a full-time Fundación Carolina Scholarship to attend her MBA in 2005. She was selected as part of the first Women in Leadership cohort in Telefonica and leveraged this experience to mentor young female professionals in the company. She was a finalist of the ‘GE Eco Imagination at Work' Competition in 2006. In addition, she won the First Prize ‘Challenge to Excellence' by Procter & Gamble in 2000, a recognition awarded to young leaders that promoted change in their communities. A keen sailor, she enjoys taking part in regattas organised by the MBA Sailing League led by top Business Schools.

Owned, led the development and effectively implemented a single Strategic Plan to ensure a step-change in resource allocation and alignment of strategic, operational and financial plans with shorter planning cycles.
